name: PyTorch FX
summary: Reed, James, Fei et al. (2021; PyTorch 1.8+): graph-based PyTorch IR for torch.compile. FX captures the forward computation as a torch.fx.GraphModule via dynamic-trace plus symbolic-shape inference. Trades expressivity for ease of adoption — traces user Python rather than requiring a constrained DSL.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: **PyTorch FX** (Reed, James, Fei, et al., 2021; PyTorch 1.8+) is the graph-based PyTorch IR for `torch.compile`. FX captures the forward computation as a `torch.fx.GraphModule`, a Python-level symbolic trace of the model, with each tensor op as a node. The distinguishing property is that **FX traces user Python code** (via dynamic-trace plus symbolic-shape inference) rather than requiring users to write in a constrained DSL.

sections.title: Trace-based vs structured IR
sections.content:
This is the same trade-off as JAX's `jit` (also trace-based) versus TVM/XLA's structured IR — FX prioritises ease of adoption over expressivity. FX is the substrate for **TorchInductor** (the `torch.compile` backend), for FX-based quantisation, partitioning, and pipeline-parallelism transformations, and for `torch.export` (the graph-export-to-stable-format path).

The limitation is that traced graphs are *specialised to the input shapes seen at trace time*; recompilation triggers when shapes change beyond marked dynamic dims.
